description: A designed vertical infographic comparing 'cooperative' versus 'uncooperative' jurisdictions on immigration detainers. The background is a dark charcoal/black gritty asphalt-like texture, with a faint photograph of a pair of dark metal handcuffs lying in the lower-center. The white headline at top reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two angled banner labels: a red banner 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the left and a blue banner 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the right, separated by a vertical divider. Under the red side a huge numeral '8 to 10' with body text: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' Under the blue side a huge numeral '2' with body text: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' Bottom-left has the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'. Style is statistic-card / comparison infographic with bold contrasting red-vs-blue color coding. Messaging technique: pressure on so-called sanctuary jurisdictions, framing non-cooperation as wasteful and dangerous and cooperation as safe/efficient. Target audience: local officials, law enforcement, and the public to build support for honoring ICE detainers.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
